We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Process Engineer with 2-5 years of experience in manufacturing and operations to join a dynamic team focused on driving operational excellence. This role involves leveraging advanced process mining and data analytics technologies to identify inefficiencies, design process improvements, and implement solutions that enhance productivity and reduce costs. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience working within complex manufacturing environments, strong problem-solving skills, and proficiency with process optimization frameworks and tools. This position offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional teams, including production, quality, and supply chain, applying data-driven insights to transform manufacturing operations.

Responsibilities  
- Analyze existing manufacturing processes using process mining tools and data analytics platforms to identify bottlenecks, waste, and opportunities for improvement  
- Collaborate with production, quality, and operations teams to design and implement process improvements that enhance throughput, reduce cycle times, and improve product quality  
- Utilize [Company] Process Mining technology alongside ERP systems such as SAP or Oracle to extract and interpret process data for actionable insights  
- Develop and maintain process documentation, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and workflow diagrams using BPMN or similar modeling standards  
- Lead root cause analysis sessions (e.g., using Six Sigma DMAIC methodology) to address operational issues and drive continuous improvement initiatives  
- Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and generate regular reports to track process performance and impact of implemented changes

jd:
Senior Logistics Manager

Location: New York, NY

About the Role:
We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Logistics Manager to join our manufacturing operations team. In this role, you will be responsible for leading and optimizing end-to-end logistics processes that support our advanced manufacturing environment. You will oversee supply chain coordination, inventory management, and distribution strategies to ensure seamless material flow and on-time delivery. The ideal candidate will bring a strong background in manufacturing logistics, proficiency with industry-standard software, and a track record of driving continuous improvement through lean and Six Sigma methodologies.

Responsibilities:
- Lead and manage all aspects of inbound and outbound logistics, including transportation, warehousing, and distribution for manufacturing operations.
- Develop and implement logistics strategies that align with production schedules to minimize lead times and reduce costs.
- Collaborate cross-functionally with procurement, production planning, and quality teams to ensure material availability and adherence to delivery commitments.
- Utilize ERP systems such as SAP or Oracle to monitor and optimize inventory levels, order fulfillment, and shipment tracking.
- Oversee third-party logistics providers (3PLs) and carrier relationships, negotiating contracts and service agreements to maximize efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
- Drive continuous improvement initiatives using Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma tools to enhance logistics workflows and reduce waste.
- Analyze logistics performance metrics (KPIs) such as on-time delivery rate, transportation costs, and inventory turnover, and implement corrective actions as needed.
- Ensure compliance with safety regulations, customs requirements, and company policies across all logistics operations.

Requirements:
- Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Industrial Engineering, Business, or a related field.
- 5-10 years of progressive experience in logistics management within a manufacturing or industrial operations environment.
- Demonstrated expertise with ERP systems (preferably SAP or Oracle) and warehouse management systems (WMS).
- Strong knowledge of supply chain principles, transportation management, inventory control, and distribution logistics.
- Proficient in Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma methodologies, with certifications such as Lean Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt preferred.
- Excellent leadership skills with experience managing cross-functional teams and third-party logistics providers.
- Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, Power BI, or Tableau.
- Familiarity with regulatory compliance related to transportation, import/export, and occupational safety standards.

Nice to Have:
- Experience with advanced supply chain technologies such as RFID, IoT-enabled asset tracking, or transportation management systems (TMS).
- Knowledge of Agile project management practices as applied to operational improvements.
- Master’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, or Business Administration.

jd:
Title: Vice President of Supply Chain  
Location: San Francisco, CA  

About the Role  
We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Vice President of Supply Chain to lead and transform our end-to-end supply chain operations within a fast-paced, technology-enabled manufacturing environment. In this senior leadership role, you will be responsible for overseeing supply chain planning, procurement, logistics, and inventory management, driving operational excellence and scalability as we expand our global footprint. You will partner closely with cross-functional teams including product, engineering, manufacturing, and finance to build a resilient, agile supply chain that leverages data analytics and best-in-class technology platforms. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of supply chain strategy in manufacturing, proven experience leading large, distributed teams, and a passion for continuous innovation and process improvement.

Responsibilities  
- Lead the development and execution of comprehensive supply chain strategies that align with business goals and support scalable manufacturing operations.  
- Oversee demand planning, procurement, supplier management, logistics, and inventory control to optimize cost, quality, and delivery performance.  
- Implement and leverage advanced supply chain technologies, including ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle Netsuite), supply chain planning tools (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder), and transportation management systems (TMS).  
- Drive end-to-end supply chain visibility and data-driven decision making through integration of advanced analytics, machine learning models, and real-time dashboards.  
- Build and mentor a high-performing supply chain leadership team, fostering a culture of operational excellence, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration.  
- Manage supplier relationships and negotiate contracts to ensure risk mitigation, cost competitiveness, and compliance with quality standards.  
- Collaborate with manufacturing and engineering teams to align supply chain capabilities with production schedules and product lifecycle management.  
- Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and sustainability initiatives across the supply chain network.  

Requirements  
- 10+ years of progressive leadership experience in supply chain management within manufacturing or operations-intensive industries, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role (Director, VP, or equivalent).  
- Proven track record of leading complex, global supply chains with multi-site manufacturing operations and diverse supplier bases.  
- Expertise in supply chain planning, procurement, logistics, inventory optimization, and supplier relationship management.  
- Hands-on experience with enterprise supply chain software platforms (SAP, Oracle Netsuite, Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, Manhattan Associates, or equivalent).  
- Strong analytical skills with proficiency in data analytics tools (Tableau, Power BI, SQL) and familiarity with applying machine learning and AI to supply chain challenges.  
- Demonstrated ability to lead large teams, drive change management initiatives, and foster a culture of continuous improvement using Lean, Six Sigma, or similar methodologies.  
- Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills, capable of influencing at all levels of the organization.  
- Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field; MBA or relevant advanced degree preferred.  

Nice to Have  
- APICS CPIM or CSCP certification or equivalent professional supply chain credentials.  
- Experience in technology-enabled or software-driven supply chain environments.  
- Knowledge of sustainability frameworks and experience driving green supply chain initiatives.  
- Familiarity with Agile methodologies in supply chain project management.

jd:
Title: Vice President of Supply Chain  
Location: San Francisco, CA  

About the Role  
We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Vice President of Supply Chain to lead and transform our end-to-end supply chain operations within a fast-paced, technology-enabled manufacturing environment. In this senior leadership role, you will be responsible for overseeing supply chain planning, procurement, logistics, and inventory management, driving operational excellence and scalability as we expand our global footprint. You will partner closely with cross-functional teams including product, engineering, manufacturing, and finance to build a resilient, agile supply chain that leverages data analytics and best-in-class technology platforms. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of supply chain strategy in manufacturing, proven experience leading large, distributed teams, and a passion for continuous innovation and process improvement.

Responsibilities  
- Lead the development and execution of comprehensive supply chain strategies that align with business goals and support scalable manufacturing operations.  
- Oversee demand planning, procurement, supplier management, logistics, and inventory control to optimize cost, quality, and delivery performance.  
- Implement and leverage advanced supply chain technologies, including ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle Netsuite), supply chain planning tools (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder), and transportation management systems (TMS).  
- Drive end-to-end supply chain visibility and data-driven decision making through integration of advanced analytics, machine learning models, and real-time dashboards.  
- Build and mentor a high-performing supply chain leadership team, fostering a culture of operational excellence, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration.  
- Manage supplier relationships and negotiate contracts to ensure risk mitigation, cost competitiveness, and compliance with quality standards.  
- Collaborate with manufacturing and engineering teams to align supply chain capabilities with production schedules and product lifecycle management.  
- Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and sustainability initiatives across the supply chain network.  

Requirements  
- 10+ years of progressive leadership experience in supply chain management within manufacturing or operations-intensive industries, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role (Director, VP, or equivalent).  
- Proven track record of leading complex, global supply chains with multi-site manufacturing operations and diverse supplier bases.  
- Expertise in supply chain planning, procurement, logistics, inventory optimization, and supplier relationship management.  
- Hands-on experience with enterprise supply chain software platforms (SAP, Oracle Netsuite, Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, Manhattan Associates, or equivalent).  
- Strong analytical skills with proficiency in data analytics tools (Tableau, Power BI, SQL) and familiarity with applying machine learning and AI to supply chain challenges.  
- Demonstrated ability to lead large teams, drive change management initiatives, and foster a culture of continuous improvement using Lean, Six Sigma, or similar methodologies.  
- Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills, capable of influencing at all levels of the organization.  
- Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field; MBA or relevant advanced degree preferred.  

Nice to Have  
- APICS CPIM or CSCP certification or equivalent professional supply chain credentials.  
- Experience in technology-enabled or software-driven supply chain environments.  
- Knowledge of sustainability frameworks and experience driving green supply chain initiatives.  
- Familiarity with Agile methodologies in supply chain project management.

jd:
Pharma Field Sales \- Cardiometabolic Care Specialist \- P Chicago East


**Facility:** Sales
**Location:**Chicago, IL, US
**About the Department**

The Cardiometabolic Care Sales Team is at the forefront of US sales efforts for Novo Nordisk’s robust cardiometabolic product portfolio, which includes world\-class therapies for treating multi\-morbid conditions such as diabetes, obesity, and the reduction of major adverse cardiovascular events. Our ambition is to advance broad cardiometabolic disease management by bringing exciting new therapies to market to improve patient outcomes. As a team member, you will connect therapies to new specialties, build cardiometabolic advocates, and apply learnings that impact local markets and the organization in a cross\-collaborative way.  



At Novo Nordisk, we create value by having a patient\-centered approach and are committed to providing innovation to the benefit of our stakeholders. We focus on personal performance and development and have a culture centered on helping leaders create the conditions for people to be at their best. If you want to join a highly diverse and collaborative team and are ready to take the next step in your career with a company committed to meeting the evolving needs of patients with cardiometabolic diseases, come join us!  


**The Position**

Assumes responsibility for achieving sales goals by implementing marketing and sales strategies aimed at effectively selling and promoting Novo Nordisk's portfolio of products to HCPs and other office staff.  


**Relationships**

Externally, the CMCS maintains relationships with physicians, physician assistants, nurse practitioners, medical assistants, pharmacists, nurses and other paramedical customers and current co\-promotion partners.  



Internally, the CMCS reports to the District Business Manager of the specific sales territory. The CMCS also interacts and collaborates on a regular basis with other field\-based employees covering the same geographic areas, particularly the territory partner.  


**Essential Functions**
* Demonstrates competencies on a consistent basis with territory level impact
* Demonstrates understanding of the local payer market including Medicare, Commercial and Medicaid benefit designs, Payer Coverage, Prescription Coverage Requirements, Step Therapy, Coverage Gap, Copays, and Deductibles and the impact on customer decisions

jd:
We're looking for a natural leader who loves cannabis and loves people — someone who can seamlessly move between delivering exceptional one\-on\-one guest experiences and keeping the floor running smoothly. If you thrive in a fast\-paced retail environment and know your terpenes from your cannabinoids, this role was built for you.

  


**Position Summary**



The Floor Lead / Budtender is a hybrid role that blends front\-line customer engagement with floor leadership responsibilities. This position is physically present on the sales floor at all times, serving as both a knowledgeable cannabis advisor to guests and a supervisory resource for the budtender team. The Floor Lead / Budtender delivers exceptional one\-on\-one service, ensures regulatory compliance, maintains an organized and welcoming retail environment, and provides real\-time guidance and support to fellow team members. This role requires a confident, detail\-oriented individual who can seamlessly transition between serving customers and leading day\-to\-day floor operations.

  


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities**



The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following:


**Customer Experience \& Sales**


* Welcome each guest personally using enthusiastic words, tone, and body language that reflects the brand.
* Verify proper identification, paperwork, and documentation for all guests prior to service.
* Work one\-on\-one with guests to recommend cannabis products that best match their preferences, needs, and desired effects.
* Provide accurate and up\-to\-date information about product inventory, pricing, promotions, strains, and consumption methods.
* Handle customer inquiries, concerns, and complaints in a professional and timely manner, striving to resolve issues to full customer satisfaction.
* Input orders in a timely and accurate manner; collect payment and make correct change.
* Quickly and precisely handle cash and debit transactions while maintaining a balanced cash drawer at all times.


**Floor Leadership \& Team Support**


* Supervise and provide real\-time guidance to budtender team members during assigned shifts.
* Ensure compliance with all applicable laws, regulations, and company policies governing the sale of cannabis products.
* Train and onboard new team members on cannabis product knowledge, POS systems, compliance requirements, and service standards.
* Motivate and support the team to maintain an effective floor atmosphere and efficient service flow.
* Conduct cash handling procedures including opening and closing registers, managing cash drops, and reconciling sales at end of shift.
* Collaborate with management to implement sales strategies, marketing initiatives, and product displays that enhance the customer experience.


**Operations \& Inventory**


* Maintain a clean, organized, and visually appealing sales floor that adheres to health and safety standards at all times.
* Clean, stock, and organize the dispensary in accordance with company standards; work with team members to keep all areas maintained throughout the shift.
* Monitor inventory levels to ensure product availability and minimize stock discrepancies.
* Conduct and/or assist with nightly inventory counts of all dispensary products.
* Update menus and signage with accurate, informative, and visually appealing content as assigned.
* Stay current on industry trends, product knowledge, and regulatory changes pertaining to cannabis.
* Perform other duties as assigned by the General Manager.


**Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities)**
-------------------------------------------------------------



Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:


* Must have flexible availability and be able to work closing and weekend shifts.
* High school diploma or equivalent required.
* Minimum one (1\) year of retail or customer service experience required; cannabis industry experience strongly preferred.
* Strong knowledge and understanding of cannabis products, strains, consumption methods, and industry regulations.
* Proven leadership abilities, including the capacity to motivate, support, and guide a team in a fast\-paced environment.
* Excellent verbal communication and interpersonal skills, with a passion for delivering exceptional customer service.
* Detail\-oriented with strong organizational and problem\-solving skills.
* Proficient in point\-of\-sale (POS) systems and basic computer applications; basic mathematical skills and ability to handle cash accurately.
* Knowledge of local and state cannabis laws and regulations where applicable.
* Must be 21 years of age or older and pass all required background checks.
* Flexibility to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as required.


**Physical Demands and Work Environment**
-----------------------------------------



The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these functions. While performing the duties of this job, an employee must walk, stand, or remain stationary for the duration of their entire shift. The employee is regularly exposed to strong cannabis odors and frequently required to handle cannabis products. Employees must be able to continuously reach, bend, lift, carry, stoop, and wipe, with the potential for slipping or tripping. Employees must frequently wash their hands and may be required to lift or move up to 50 pounds.

jd:
At Gilead, we’re creating a healthier world for all people. For more than 35 years, we’ve tackled diseases such as HIV, viral hepatitis, COVID\-19 and cancer – working relentlessly to develop therapies that help improve lives and to ensure access to these therapies across the globe. We continue to fight against the world’s biggest health challenges, and our mission requires collaboration, determination and a relentless drive to make a difference.

  

Every member of Gilead’s team plays a critical role in the discovery and development of life\-changing scientific innovations. Our employees are our greatest asset as we work to achieve our bold ambitions, and we’re looking for the next wave of passionate and ambitious people ready to make a direct impact.

  

We believe every employee deserves a great leader. People Leaders are the cornerstone to the employee experience at Gilead and Kite. As a people leader now or in the future, you are the key driver in evolving our culture and creating an environment where every employee feels included, developed and empowered to fulfil their aspirations. Join Gilead and help create possible, together.  

  

**Job Description**

  

At Gilead our pursuit of a healthier world for all people has yielded a cure for hepatitis C, revolutionary improvements in HIV treatment and prevention as well as advancements in therapies for viral and inflammatory diseases and certain cancers.


We set and achieve bold ambitions in our fight against the world’s most devastating diseases, united in our commitment to confronting the largest public health challenges of our day and improving the lives of patients for generations to come.


We are seeking an exceptional, experienced, and strategic senior communications leader to help shape and execute Gilead’s commercial communications and executive narrative during high\-impact business moments. The role is responsible for developing and executing a proactive, integrated communications strategy that support Commercial priorities, including product launches, market expansion, performance milestones, and other critical business initiatives.


The **Senior Director, Strategic Communications** serves as a key and senior advisor and collaborator to the Chief Commercial Officer and key partners across Commercial, Public Affairs, Legal, Finance, Government Affairs, and other functions. Acting as a trusted consultant, the role shapes and advances Gilead’s commercial narrative and strategy, business performance messaging, and market positioning, ensuring alignment with business objectives, market realities, and clearly articulate Gilead’s value to customers, partners, and stakeholders.


Operating with a high degree of autonomy, the ideal candidate brings deep experience in executive and enterprise communications and a strong understanding of commercial strategy and market dynamics. The role leads through expertise and influence, translating complex business, scientific, and operational topics into clear, compelling executive\-level communications that support business priorities, organizational change, emerging issues and reputational considerations.


This role will lead a coordinated 360° communications approach and strategy in partnership with Gilead’s Communications Center of Excellence, supporting executive visibility and engagement tied to commercial priorities. The role will report to the Vice President, Corporate Communications and partner closely with Commercial leadership.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Strategic Commercial Communications: Develop and execute integrated communications strategies that support commercial priorities and during business\-critical moments, including product launches, market expansion, key business milestones, organizational change and emerging issues.
* Executive Counsel: Serve as a primary strategic and trusted advisor to the Chief Commercial Officer and Commercial Leadership Team on business positioning, narrative development, stakeholder engagement, and reputational considerations.
* Content Development: Lead the creation, review, and refinement of executive\-level materials including speeches, business updates, town halls, talking points, Q\&A documents, and external thought leadership that support long\-range commercial priorities.
* Commercial Strategy Translation: Translate commercial strategy, performance priorities, and market dynamics into clear and compelling communications aligned with business objectives.
* Anticipate risks and opportunities, independently identifying communications needs and proactively shaping executive narratives.
* 360° Communications Activation: Partner across the Communications Center of Excellence, including Internal Communications, Media Relations, Digital and Social, and Issues Management, to ensure coordinated execution across all channels.
* Issues and Business\-Critical Communications: Support communications strategy and execution during sensitive or business\-critical situations impacting the Commercial organization.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Act as a central liaison across Commercial, Public Affairs, Legal, Finance, and other functions to ensure alignment and consistent communications.
* Serve as a senior communications consultant and representative on highly significant matters related to commercial strategy, messaging, and executive visibility.
* Executive Preparation: Support executive preparation and coaching for internal and external engagements, including industry events, media interactions, and leadership forums.
* Measurement and Insights: Leverage data, stakeholder insights, and performance metrics to assess and refine communications strategies.
* Operate with minimal direction, exercising sound judgment in sensitive, highvisibility, or reputationally significant situations.
* Provide recommendations that often influence financial, employee, and public perception outcomes.
* Agency Management: Manage external agencies and advisors supporting strategic communications programs and commercial milestones.

 **Skills, Knowledge and Expectations**

* Demonstrated and deep expertise supporting executive, strategic or commercial communications within a global, matrixed organization.
* Viewed as a subject matter expert capable of influencing senior leaders through credibility, insight, and judgment.
* Demonstrated ability to develop solutions to complex, nonroutine problems that may set precedent for future decisions.
* Strong understanding of commercial strategy, product lifecycle, and market dynamics, with healthcare or biopharma experience strongly preferred.
* Proven ability to translate complex business priorities into clear and effective communications.
* Experience supporting executive visibility across internal and external channels.
* Exceptional strategic writing and editing skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information rapidly and clearly.
* Sound judgment and composure in managing sensitive or high\-impact situations.
* Outstanding interpersonal and relationship\-building skills.
* Experience leading through influence and driving alignment across cross\-functional teams.
* Proven ability to operate independently, manage ambiguity, and deliver highimpact outcomes through influence rather than authority.
* Demonstrated ability to manage agency partners and complex communications programs.

 **Basic Qualifications**


Bachelor’s degree and 14\+ years of experience, or
  

Master’s degree and 12\+ years of experience, or
  

12\+ years of experience in strategic communications, executive communications, corporate affairs, or related roles within a publicly traded company or agency environment; healthcare or biopharmaceutical experience strongly preferred.

**People Leader Accountabilities:**

* Create Inclusion \- knowing the business value of diverse teams, modeling inclusion, and embedding the value of diversity in the


way they manage their teams.

* Develop Talent \- understand the skills, experience, aspirations and potential of their employees and coach them on current


performance and future potential. They ensure employees are receiving the feedback and insight needed to grow, develop and


realize their purpose.

* Empower Teams \- connect the team to the organization by aligning goals, purpose, and organizational objectives, and holding


them to account. They provide the support needed to remove barriers and connect their team to the broader ecosystem.

  


The salary range for this position is: $243,100\.00 \- $314,600\.00\. Gilead considers a variety of factors when determining base compensation, including experience, qualifications, and geographic location. These considerations mean actual compensation will vary. This position may also be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, discretionary stock\-based long\-term incentives (eligibility may vary based on role), paid time off, and a benefits package. Benefits include company\-sponsored medical, dental, vision, and life insurance plans\*.
For additional benefits information, visit:


https://www.gilead.com/careers/compensation\-benefits\-and\-wellbeing

* Eligible employees may participate in benefit plans, subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable plans.

For more than 35 years, we’ve tackled diseases such as HIV, viral hepatitis, COVID\-19 and cancer – working relentlessly to develop therapies that help improve lives and to ensure access to these therapies across the globe. We continue to fight against the world’s biggest health challenges, and our mission requires collaboration, determination and a relentless drive to make a difference.

  

Every member of Gilead’s team plays a critical role in the discovery and development of life\-changing scientific innovations. Our employees are our greatest asset as we work to achieve our bold ambitions, and we’re looking for the next wave of passionate and ambitious people ready to make a direct impact.

  

We believe every employee deserves a great leader. People Leaders are the cornerstone to the employee experience at Gilead and Kite. As a people leader now or in the future, you are the key driver in evolving our culture and creating an environment where every employee feels included, developed and empowered to fulfil their aspirations. Join Gilead and help create possible, together.  

  

**Job Description**

  

At Gilead our pursuit of a healthier world for all people has yielded a cure for hepatitis C, revolutionary improvements in HIV treatment and prevention as well as advancements in therapies for viral and inflammatory diseases and certain cancers.


We set and achieve bold ambitions in our fight against the world’s most devastating diseases, united in our commitment to confronting the largest public health challenges of our day and improving the lives of patients for generations to come.


We are seeking an exceptional, experienced, and strategic senior communications leader to help shape and execute Gilead’s commercial communications and executive narrative during high\-impact business moments. The role is responsible for developing and executing a proactive, integrated communications strategy that support Commercial priorities, including product launches, market expansion, performance milestones, and other critical business initiatives.


The **Senior Director, Strategic Communications** serves as a key and senior advisor and collaborator to the Chief Commercial Officer and key partners across Commercial, Public Affairs, Legal, Finance, Government Affairs, and other functions. Acting as a trusted consultant, the role shapes and advances Gilead’s commercial narrative and strategy, business performance messaging, and market positioning, ensuring alignment with business objectives, market realities, and clearly articulate Gilead’s value to customers, partners, and stakeholders.


Operating with a high degree of autonomy, the ideal candidate brings deep experience in executive and enterprise communications and a strong understanding of commercial strategy and market dynamics. The role leads through expertise and influence, translating complex business, scientific, and operational topics into clear, compelling executive\-level communications that support business priorities, organizational change, emerging issues and reputational considerations.


This role will lead a coordinated 360° communications approach and strategy in partnership with Gilead’s Communications Center of Excellence, supporting executive visibility and engagement tied to commercial priorities. The role will report to the Vice President, Corporate Communications and partner closely with Commercial leadership.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Strategic Commercial Communications: Develop and execute integrated communications strategies that support commercial priorities and during business\-critical moments, including product launches, market expansion, key business milestones, organizational change and emerging issues.
* Executive Counsel: Serve as a primary strategic and trusted advisor to the Chief Commercial Officer and Commercial Leadership Team on business positioning, narrative development, stakeholder engagement, and reputational considerations.
* Content Development: Lead the creation, review, and refinement of executive\-level materials including speeches, business updates, town halls, talking points, Q\&A documents, and external thought leadership that support long\-range commercial priorities.
* Commercial Strategy Translation: Translate commercial strategy, performance priorities, and market dynamics into clear and compelling communications aligned with business objectives.
* Anticipate risks and opportunities, independently identifying communications needs and proactively shaping executive narratives.
* 360° Communications Activation: Partner across the Communications Center of Excellence, including Internal Communications, Media Relations, Digital and Social, and Issues Management, to ensure coordinated execution across all channels.
* Issues and Business\-Critical Communications: Support communications strategy and execution during sensitive or business\-critical situations impacting the Commercial organization.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Act as a central liaison across Commercial, Public Affairs, Legal, Finance, and other functions to ensure alignment and consistent communications.
* Serve as a senior communications consultant and representative on highly significant matters related to commercial strategy, messaging, and executive visibility.
* Executive Preparation: Support executive preparation and coaching for internal and external engagements, including industry events, media interactions, and leadership forums.
* Measurement and Insights: Leverage data, stakeholder insights, and performance metrics to assess and refine communications strategies.
* Operate with minimal direction, exercising sound judgment in sensitive, highvisibility, or reputationally significant situations.
* Provide recommendations that often influence financial, employee, and public perception outcomes.
* Agency Management: Manage external agencies and advisors supporting strategic communications programs and commercial milestones.

 **Skills, Knowledge and Expectations**

* Demonstrated and deep expertise supporting executive, strategic or commercial communications within a global, matrixed organization.
* Viewed as a subject matter expert capable of influencing senior leaders through credibility, insight, and judgment.
* Demonstrated ability to develop solutions to complex, nonroutine problems that may set precedent for future decisions.
* Strong understanding of commercial strategy, product lifecycle, and market dynamics, with healthcare or biopharma experience strongly preferred.
* Proven ability to translate complex business priorities into clear and effective communications.
* Experience supporting executive visibility across internal and external channels.
* Exceptional strategic writing and editing skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information rapidly and clearly.
* Sound judgment and composure in managing sensitive or high\-impact situations.
* Outstanding interpersonal and relationship\-building skills.
* Experience leading through influence and driving alignment across cross\-functional teams.
* Proven ability to operate independently, manage ambiguity, and deliver highimpact outcomes through influence rather than authority.
* Demonstrated ability to manage agency partners and complex communications programs.

 **Basic Qualifications**


Bachelor’s degree and 14\+ years of experience, or
  

Master’s degree and 12\+ years of experience, or
  

12\+ years of experience in strategic communications, executive communications, corporate affairs, or related roles within a publicly traded company or agency environment; healthcare or biopharmaceutical experience strongly preferred.

**People Leader Accountabilities:**

* Create Inclusion \- knowing the business value of diverse teams, modeling inclusion, and embedding the value of diversity in the


way they manage their teams.

* Develop Talent \- understand the skills, experience, aspirations and potential of their employees and coach them on current


performance and future potential. They ensure employees are receiving the feedback and insight needed to grow, develop and


realize their purpose.

* Empower Teams \- connect the team to the organization by aligning goals, purpose, and organizational objectives, and holding


them to account. They provide the support needed to remove barriers and connect their team to the broader ecosystem.

  


The salary range for this position is: $243,100\.00 \- $314,600\.00\. Gilead considers a variety of factors when determining base compensation, including experience, qualifications, and geographic location. These considerations mean actual compensation will vary. This position may also be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, discretionary stock\-based long\-term incentives (eligibility may vary based on role), paid time off, and a benefits package. Benefits include company\-sponsored medical, dental, vision, and life insurance plans\*.
For additional benefits information, visit:


https://www.gilead.com/careers/compensation\-benefits\-and\-wellbeing

* Eligible employees may participate in benefit plans, subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable plans.

**For jobs in the United States:**
----------------------------------


[Company] Inc. is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment, and is dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment comprised of diverse perspectives, backgrounds, and experiences. Employment decisions regarding recruitment and selection will be made without discrimination based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, genetic information or characteristic, gender identity and expression, veteran status, or other non\-job related characteristics or other prohibited grounds specified in applicable federal, state and local laws. In order to ensure reasonable accommodation for individuals protected by Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Vietnam Era Veterans' Readjustment Act of 1974, and Title I of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, applicants who require accommodation in the job application process may contact ApplicantAccommodations@gilead.com for assistance.

  

For more information about equal employment opportunity protections, please view the 'Know Your Rights' poster.


NOTICE: EMPLOYEE POLYGRAPH PROTECTION ACT  

YOUR RIGHTS UNDER THE FAMILY AND MEDICAL LEAVE ACT

  

[Company] will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant. However, employees who have access to the compensation information of other employees or applicants as a part of their essential job functions cannot disclose the pay of other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise have access to compensation information, unless the disclosure is (a) in response to a formal complaint or charge, (b) in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted by the employer, (c) consistent with the legal duty to furnish information; or (d) otherwise protected by law.

  

Our environment respects individual differences and recognizes each employee as an integral member of our company. Our workforce reflects these values and celebrates the individuals who make up our growing team.

  

Gilead provides a work environment free of harassment and prohibited conduct. We promote and support individual differences and diversity of thoughts and opinion.

jd:
**WHO ARE YOU?** You have grown a passion for building better digital learning products and your tech\-savvy abilities mean you can easily turn user needs into clear priorities and specs. To keep decisions moving and deliverables on track, you know what it takes to be a trusted partner working across the aisle to bring engineering, UX, and content/design teams. **WHO ARE WE?** We are passionate, innovative, lifelong learners, and creative thinkers working to develop culturally authentic language learning products for K\-12 schools and universities.  

**WHAT IS THE ROLE ABOUT?** As the Technical Product Owner, you will prioritize and deliver our internal activity and assessment tools, including VHL Central’s IGC and games. You will partner with stakeholders to set priorities, manage the product backlog, and ensure business, user, and technical needs are met. Serving as the primary liaison, you will work closely with engineering, UX, and content/design, to clarify intent, remove constraints, and guide day\-to\-day decisions while balancing near\-term execution with long\-term platform goals.  

**IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:*** Own the product strategy and backlog for Digital Authoring Workflow tools and content experiences, ensuring work is well\-defined, prioritized, and feasible
* Translate business and user needs into technical requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria that guide delivery
* Make day\-to\-day product decisions to keep development moving, balancing near\-term execution with the product vision, roadmap, and technical strategy
* Manage relationships with external vendors and partners to support digital product development and delivery
* Drive continuous improvement in tools, workflows, and delivery practices to improve quality, speed, and operational efficiency
* Partner with engineering, UX, and content/design to deliver intuitive experiences; define and track KPIs, and share insights with leadership to shape the future direction

 **YOU MUST HAVE (MINIMUM REQUIRED SKILLS \& EXPERIENCE):*** Bachelor’s degree.
* 3\+ years of experience in a Technical Product Owner or Manager or similar role specifically working with EdTech or educational publishing content creation tooling and design.
* Effective project management skills and experience with cross\-functional stakeholders including engineering, UX, and content teams to translate user needs and editorial intent into clear, feasible, and high\-quality product solutions
* Well\-developed product strategy ownership skills and multi quarter roadmaps for complex digital platforms, content authoring, or learning technology environments
* Experience effectively leading Agile/Scrum teams strategically driving workflow transformation and change management, particularly moving teams toward digital first tools and processes
* Advanced understanding of and experience developing content authoring tooling for EdTech landscape, and market demands in K–12 and/or higher education sectors
* Strong proficiency in Atlassian tools (Jira, Confluence) and Figma, Miro, and Lucid for wireframing, prototyping, and cross\-functional brainstorming
* Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace for digital product planning, documentation, stakeholder communication, and project tracking

jd:
The US Consumer Services (USCS) group is responsible for growing our consumer business worldwide, strengthening our global leadership position in the premium and travel space, and delivering exceptional, differentiated customer experiences.


Within USCS’ Travel \& Lifestyle (TLS) team, the Global Lounge Experiences team is a high impact group, responsible for the strategic definition and delivery of the Global Lounge Collection benefit to Amex’s global premium Card Members. The Global Lounge Collection benefit encompasses the Centurion Lounge network and access to network lounges through partnerships (i.e., Delta, Priority Pass).


The Centurion Lounge Product Management team is responsible for managing the customer experience and defining the ongoing strategy for the Centurion Lounge network. This team member will play a key role in owning two key parts of the team strategy and customer experience: access policies and the quality assurance program. He/she will be responsible for managing all access policies, including the ongoing management of our Lounge Guest Access Policy. In addition, he/she will own the Centurion Lounge Quality Assurance program, leveraging multiple sources to measure the resonance of the Centurion Lounge experience, and extract key highlights and opportunities to inform future product and operational innovation.


  
* 2\-3 years of relevant experience preferred
* Bachelor's required
* Comfortable driving results from semi\-structured guidelines
* Intimate knowledge of Amex internal marketing systems including DAM, etc.
* Solutions\-oriented, strategic thinker with attention to detail
* Strong cross\-functional partner management skills
* Strong verbal and written communications skills
* Passion for and/or thorough understanding of the premium travel landscape and marketing
* Ability to identify opportunities proactively and independently for improvement and drive results
* Team player with a passion to create the best customer experience for our Card Members
* Confidence, personal presence, and comfort interacting with all levels of the organization
* Ability to stay calm under pressure and flexible amidst change
* Ability to seamlessly juggle multiple projects and priorities
* Employment eligibility to work with [Company] in the U.S. is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for these positions.

  
At [Company], our culture is built on a 175\-year history of innovation, shared values and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. From delivering differentiated products to providing world\-class customer service, we operate with a strong risk mindset, ensuring we continue to uphold our brand promise of trust, security, and service.


As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well\-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of [Company].


  
We back you with benefits that support your holistic well\-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:


* Competitive base salaries
* Bonus incentives
* 6% Company Match on retirement savings plan
* Free financial coaching and financial well\-being support
* Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits
* Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need
* 20\+ weeks paid parental leave for all parents, regardless of gender, offered for pregnancy, adoption or surrogacy
* Free access to global on\-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location)
* Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program
* Career development and training opportunities

For a full list of Team Amex benefits, visit out Colleague Benefits Site .


[Company] is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, disability status, age, or any other status protected by law. [Company] will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with arrest or conviction records, in accordance with the requirements of applicable state and local laws, including the California Fair Chance Act, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the City of Los Angeles’ Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance. For positions covered by federal and/or state banking regulations, [Company] will comply with such regulations as it relates to the consideration of applicants with criminal convictions.  

  

We back our colleagues with the support they need to thrive, professionally and personally. That's why we have Amex Flex, our enterprise working model that provides greater flexibility to colleagues while ensuring we preserve the important aspects of our unique in\-person culture. Depending on role and business needs, colleagues will either work onsite, in a hybrid model (combination of in\-office and virtual days) or fully virtually.

jd:
Reports To: VP of Product

Location: Remote US

Compensation: $230,000 to $250,000 
base plus bonus and equity

 

What We Do:

[Company] is a fully remote, global team of passionate experts and ethical badasses on a mission to break down the barriers to cybersecurity. Whether creating purpose-built security solutions, hunting down hackers, or impacting our community, our people go above and beyond to change the security game and make a real difference.

Founded in 2015 by former NSA cyber operators, [Company] protects all businesses—not just the 1%—with enterprise-grade, fully owned, and managed cybersecurity products at the price of an affordable SaaS application. The [Company] difference is our One Team advantage: our technology is designed with our industry-defining Security Operations Center (SOC) in mind and is never separated from our service.

We protect 4M+ endpoints and 7M+ identities worldwide, elevating underresourced IT teams with protection that works as hard as they do. As long as hackers keep hacking, [Company] keeps hunting.

About the Role:

We are building a product management organization to own and drive our product strategy and are looking for an experienced product manager to join this growing team. As a Staff Product Manager at [Company], you will assist the CTO in driving the success and expansion of our SIEM product launched in October 2024. We are not building yet another SIEM that intends to replicate what every other SIEM already does. We are building a SIEM that focuses on what really matters to our Small Enterprise and SMB partners. They don’t need a SIEM with all the advanced features and the costs that come with that; they need a SIEM that keeps the relevant data, reduces the noise, makes it easy to find what they need, doesn’t require full-time employees to manage, and works at a cost that fits their budget. This is an opportunity to upend the existing SIEM market for everyone who wants or needs a SIEM but whose current solutions don’t work.

This position offers the chance to help scale the vision of an already wildly successful company past $200M and beyond, sustaining and building on a remarkable existing growth rate. You will make an impact on the vision, goals, and, in partnership with other functions, execution of a SaaS offering (like other [Company] products) that keeps SMB and mid-market customers safe and secure with cutting-edge capabilities currently only found in the largest enterprises. You will work very closely with the company founders, product leaders, engineering leaders, support, sales, and marketing teams, as well as other Product Managers.  

To be successful in this position, you must possess a combination of strong business acumen and technical skills to bridge customer business requirements with trends in the threat landscape. You’ll be asked to frequently interact with customers, prospects, and industry analysts to articulate our value proposition.

What You’ll Do:

Work with the Portfolio Leader and relevant stakeholders to assist in setting the SIEM product vision and strategy, and clearly articulate the business value so that everyone understands where we are headed

Analyze market trends and perform competitor analysis and research in order to drive strategy as well as pricing and the product roadmap

Demonstrate an ability to decipher and articulate potentially ambiguous client needs and mold them into successful, innovative solutions that surprise and delight the customer

Work with designers, engineers, and architects to create detailed business requirements, epics, and user stories that will be used to shape product specifications and architecture 

Prioritize features by assessing their alignment with the broader product strategy and initiatives, as well as unmet market needs

Participate as a key contributor in all phases of product development, from discovery to launch

Drive and manage product roadmap and go-to-market strategy by working closely with marketing, sales, and executive leadership teams

Collaborate with marketing and other key stakeholders to drive and execute product launches to market

Derive feedback and insights from customers through face-to-face interaction and collaboration throughout the product development lifecycle

Serve as the intermediary between engineering and senior management and other non-technical stakeholders, with the ability to break down and translate highly technical information into layman's terms and vice versa

Drive high-level strategic initiatives and execution while possessing a willingness to dig into the weeds

Work with design and engineering teams to produce mockups, wireframes, and prototypes to assess the validity and strength of a new product or feature idea.

Manage and drive results from user experience testing and gain crucial insights into how customers use (or don’t use) the platform

In partnership with product operations, provide product metrics, including conversion rate, churn rate, and monthly recurring revenue, etc., to track and evaluate the success of the product strategy

What You Bring To The Team:  

7+ years of experience in a product management role with past responsibility for SIEM products; bonus for SOAR Products

Demonstrated success driving longer-term vision and strategy that resulted in outperformance

Experience leading product innovation, feature ideation, delivery, and launch

Demonstrated experience designing and delivering products utilizing artificial intelligence tools and technologies, such as Claude or similar

Experience working in an agile development environment using agile development tools 

Leverage excellent written and verbal communication skills to create epics and stories for engineering that align engineers with intended outcomes

Experience with products delivered from AWS or other cloud environments

Strong creative and critical thinking abilities

Experience building products for the SMB is a significant benefit
 

What We Offer: 

100% remote work environment - since our founding in 2015

Generous paid time off policy, including vacation, sick time, and paid holidays

12 weeks of paid parental leave

Highly competitive and comprehensive medical, dental, and vision benefits plans 

401(k) with a 5% contribution regardless of employee contribution

Life and Disability insurance plans

Stock options for 
all
full-time employees 

One-time $500 reimbursement for building/upgrading home office

Annual allowance for education and professional development assistance 

$75 USD/month digital reimbursement

Access to the BetterUp platform for coaching, personal, and professional growth

  

[Company] is committed to creating a culture of inclusivity where every single member of our team is valued, has a voice, and is empowered to come to work every day just as they are.
 

We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, veteran status, genetic information, marital status, or any other legally protected status. 
 

We do discriminate against hackers who try to exploit businesses of all sizes.

accommodations@huntresslabs.com
. Please note that non-accommodation requests to this inbox will not receive a response.

jd:
**Requisition I****D****:** 924080  

**Store \#:** A00083 eComm \- NY 5th  

**Position**: Full\-Time  

**Total Rewards:** **Benefits/Incentive Information**  



If you’ve worn a pair of glasses, we’ve already met.

We are a global leader in the design, manufacture, and distribution of ophthalmic lenses, frames, and sunglasses. We offer our industry stakeholders in over 150 countries access to a global platform of high\-quality vision care products such as the Essilor brand, with Varilux, Crizal, Eyezen, Stellest and Transitions, iconic brands that consumers love such as Ray\-Ban, Oakley, Persol and Oliver Peoples, as well as a network that offers consumers high\-quality vision care and best\-in\-class shopping experiences such as Sunglass Hut, LensCrafters, and Target Optical, and leading e\-commerce platforms.

With a unique global network of commercial subsidiaries and independent distributors across major markets, our customers are offered a strong portfolio of the most popular lens, frames, instruments and equipment brands that can serve every sector of the market. The Professional Solutions team works with our customers one\-on\-one, developing professional relationships based on trust and care.

Join our global community of over 200,000 dedicated employees around the world in driving the transformation of the eyewear and eyecare industry. Discover more by following us on LinkedIn!  



GENERAL FUNCTION

The Account Manager, Ecommerce Channel plays a critical role in our online growth strategy for Wholesale. This requires the ability to manage a diverse portfolio of online pure players across our fashion and optical specialist segments, while aligning with overall business objectives. The Account Manager will be able to utilize the resources of internal teams such as operations, supply chain/planning, credit and IT to provide support to accounts. They will be able to leverage merchandising and analysis skills to drive sell\-in and sell\-out of brands. The Account Manager will develop a strategic partnership with their portfolio accounts that delivers on objectives in net sales, profitability, market share, and brand marketing.

MAJOR DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

PLANNING \& BUDGETING
* Partner with sales team to develop annual budgets and quarterly forecasts.
* Develop agendas and presentations for market meetings.


PRODUCT ASSORTMENT
* Collaborate with accounts to optimize product assortment, including NPI selection and optimization of assortment based on sell\-through data
* Partner with Ecommerce Project Manager and Brand teams to structure opportunities for expansion.
* Support the sell\-in and sell\-through of brand items or collections strategic for brands.


MARKETING \& PROMOTIONAL STRATEGY
* Partner with accounts and marketing to identify opportunities that can deliver strong return on ad spend.
* Work with accounts to analyze sell\-through to measure impact of marketing investments.


PLANNING \& INVENTORY MANAGEMENT
* Support buyers for fashion accounts with order writing to ensure consistent reorders and improve sell\-through of brands.
* Use understanding of customer and assortment strategies place pre\-procurements for NPI.
* Partner with accounts and supply chain to place procurements to ensure inventory availability through forecasting.


COMMUNICATION
* Communicates confidently, and professionally with clients and external partners. Tailors the message to each stakeholder’s needs, ensuring clarity and alignment.
* Build strong, trust\-based relationships that support long\-term business growth.
* Communicate with cross\-functional internal teams and convey client expectations, project priorities, and deadlines to ensure seamless execution.
* Communicates proactively with the manager, providing updates on client status, project progress, risks, and opportunities.


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS
* 5\+ years’ experience in sales, merchandising or ecommerce.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
* Digital Savvy: Has a proficient understanding of the shopper journey via both web and marketplace (Amazon). Understands how brands use marketing to acquire and retain consumers.
* Analytical: Believe in data\-driven decision making. Ready to roll up sleeves to understand the data and find opportunities.
* Passionate: Belief in the power and integrity of our brands. Able to persist through roadblocks to get things done, leveraging network and internal/external relationships.
* Strong communicator: Capable to craft clear and compelling presentations and communications.
* Driven: Pushes self and others to find the path forward and does not give up until goals are achieved.
* Solution\-oriented to support fast decision\-making.
* Strong PC skills, including Excel, Word and PowerPoint

  

Pay Range: 98,982\.40 \- 155,017\.60

This posting is for an existing vacancy within our business. Employee pay is determined by multiple factors, including geography, experience, qualifications, skills and local minimum wage requirements. In addition, you may also be offered a competitive bonus and/or commission plan, which complements a first\-class total rewards package. Benefits may include health care, retirement savings, paid time off/vacation, and various employee discounts.  



[Company] complies with all applicable laws related to the application and hiring process. If you would like to provide feedback regarding an active job posting, or if you are an individual with a disability who would like to request a reasonable accommodation, please call the [Company] SpeakUp Hotline at 844\-303\-0229 (be sure to provide your name, job id number, and contact information so that we may follow up in a timely manner) or email HRCompliance@luxotticaretail.com.  



We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, gender, national origin, social origin, social condition, being perceived as a victim of domestic violence, sexual aggression or stalking, religion, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, ancestry, veteran or military status, marital status, pregnancy (including unlawful discrimination on the basis of a legally protected pregnancy or maternity leave), genetic information or any other characteristics protected by law. Native Americans in the US receive preference in accordance with Tribal Law.

The Account Manager will develop a strategic partnership with their portfolio accounts that delivers on objectives in net sales, profitability, market share, and brand marketing.

MAJOR DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

PLANNING \& BUDGETING
* Partner with sales team to develop annual budgets and quarterly forecasts.
* Develop agendas and presentations for market meetings.


PRODUCT ASSORTMENT
* Collaborate with accounts to optimize product assortment, including NPI selection and optimization of assortment based on sell\-through data
* Partner with Ecommerce Project Manager and Brand teams to structure opportunities for expansion.
* Support the sell\-in and sell\-through of brand items or collections strategic for brands.


MARKETING \& PROMOTIONAL STRATEGY
* Partner with accounts and marketing to identify opportunities that can deliver strong return on ad spend.
* Work with accounts to analyze sell\-through to measure impact of marketing investments.


PLANNING \& INVENTORY MANAGEMENT
* Support buyers for fashion accounts with order writing to ensure consistent reorders and improve sell\-through of brands.
* Use understanding of customer and assortment strategies place pre\-procurements for NPI.
* Partner with accounts and supply chain to place procurements to ensure inventory availability through forecasting.


COMMUNICATION
* Communicates confidently, and professionally with clients and external partners. Tailors the message to each stakeholder’s needs, ensuring clarity and alignment.
* Build strong, trust\-based relationships that support long\-term business growth.
* Communicate with cross\-functional internal teams and convey client expectations, project priorities, and deadlines to ensure seamless execution.
* Communicates proactively with the manager, providing updates on client status, project progress, risks, and opportunities.


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS
* 5\+ years’ experience in sales, merchandising or ecommerce.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
* Digital Savvy: Has a proficient understanding of the shopper journey via both web and marketplace (Amazon). Understands how brands use marketing to acquire and retain consumers.
* Analytical: Believe in data\-driven decision making. Ready to roll up sleeves to understand the data and find opportunities.
* Passionate: Belief in the power and integrity of our brands. Able to persist through roadblocks to get things done, leveraging network and internal/external relationships.
* Strong communicator: Capable to craft clear and compelling presentations and communications.
* Driven: Pushes self and others to find the path forward and does not give up until goals are achieved.
* Solution\-oriented to support fast decision\-making.
* Strong PC skills, including Excel, Word and PowerPoint

  

Pay Range: 98,982\.40 \- 155,017\.60

jd:
Our sales team is committed to customer centricity by focusing on understanding and meeting the unique needs and aspirations of each customer. We prioritize delivering exceptional value through tailored solutions and strategic partnerships. By fostering a collaborative environment across all go-to-market functions, we engage in value selling, ensuring that every interaction enhances and maximizes the customer experience. By adopting an inclusive approach, we work closely with marketing, product, and customer success teams to ensure alignment and continuous improvement. Together, we identify and overcome technical, educational, and competitive challenges, thereby driving successful, long-term growth and customer satisfaction.

We’re looking for a motivated and curious individual to help us grow our footprint across the EMEA region. Our sales team is committed to customer centricity by focusing on understanding and meeting the unique needs and aspirations of each customer. We prioritize delivering exceptional value through tailored solutions and strategic partnerships. By fostering a collaborative environment across all go-to-market functions, we engage in value selling, ensuring that every interaction enhances and maximizes the customer experience. You will work closely with our team of Account Executives to execute strategic outbound plays that are targeted and customer-centric, serving as the first point of contact to build trust and deliver value for our future customers.

This role is based in our Dublin office with an office-centric hybrid schedule. The standard in-office days are Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday. Most [Company]s have the option to work from home on Wednesdays. Working from home on Fridays depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner. If you're interviewing for this role, your recruiter will share more about the in-office requirements.

What you’ll achieve

Create EMEA pipeline by identifying, researching, and engaging high-potential prospects across key regions.

Execute targeted, multi-channel outbound prospecting including email, phone, and social to generate qualified opportunities.

Master [Company]’s product, value proposition, and ideal customer profile to effectively articulate our impact and tailor outreach to customer needs.

Partner directly with your aligned Account Executives to effectively build quality pipeline and drive customer acquisition.

Consistently meet and exceed weekly and monthly activity metrics, opportunity targets, and pipeline generation targets.

Develop foundational sales skills and business acumen to support your long-term career growth within [Company].

About you

Fluent in French.

1+ year of experience in inbound or outbound sales development, or a relevant customer-facing role.

Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.

Effective communication skills, empathy, and a strong commitment to integrity.

An analytical thinker who can identify patterns across data sets to optimize outreach.

A team player who thrives on cross-functional collaboration and shared success.

Enthusiastic about technology and eager to share how [Company] can help teams work together effortlessly.

At [Company], we're committed to building teams that include a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills, as this is critical to helping us achieve our mission. If you're interested in this role and don't meet every listed requirement, we still encourage you to apply.

What we’ll offer

Our comprehensive compensation package plays a big part in how we recognize you for the impact you have on our path to achieving our mission. We believe that compensation should be reflective of the value you create relative to the market value of your role. To ensure pay is fair and not impacted by biases, we're committed to looking at market value which is why we check ourselves and conduct a yearly pay equity audit.

For this role, the On-Target Earnings (OTE) range is €
58,000 - €65,000
 (based on the terms of the Sales Incentive Plan). These ranges are a guideline; actual base salary and OTE may vary based on various factors, including market and individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process, and the ranges for this role may be modified.

In addition, your compensation package may include additional components such as equity and benefits. If you're interviewing for this role, speak with your recruiter to learn more about the total compensation and benefits for this role.

We strive to provide equitable and competitive benefits packages that support our employees worldwide and include:

Mental health, wellness & fitness benefits

Career coaching & support

Inclusive family building benefits

Long-term savings or retirement plans

In-office culinary options to cater to your dietary preferences

These are just some of the benefits we offer, and benefits may vary based on role, country, and local regulations. If you're interviewing for this role, speak with your recruiter to learn more about the total compensation and benefits for this role.

jd:
[Company] is reinventing credit to make it more honest and friendly, giving consumers the flexibility to buy now and pay later without any hidden fees or compounding interest.
On the ML Fraud team, you’ll build and improve machine learning systems that make real-time transaction decisions, protecting consumers and merchants while balancing fraud loss, customer experience, and conversion. You’ll work closely with experienced ML engineers, platform partners, and cross-functional stakeholders to take models from idea to prototype to production, and to keep them healthy with strong measurement and monitoring as fraud patterns evolve.

 

What you’ll do

- You will develop and iterate on fraud prediction models using a mix of approaches for tabular and behavioral data

- You will build and scale feature pipelines and training datasets from proprietary and third-party signals, partnering with data and platform teams when needed.

- You will prototype new modeling ideas and features, run offline experiments, and drive the best-performing approaches into production with appropriate risk controls.

- You will help productionize models: integrate into batch and/or real-time decision systems, and improve reliability, latency, and operational robustness.

- You will instrument and monitor model and data health, and help define retraining/backtesting workflows as fraud patterns evolve.

- You will collaborate across Engineering, Fraud Analytics, Product, and ML Platform to define requirements, evaluate tradeoffs, and communicate results clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

 

What we look for

- You have a total of 2+ years of experience as a machine learning engineer or a PhD in a relevant field.

- Strong Python skills and experience writing production-quality code.

- Experience building and evaluating models for tabular classification problems (preferably gradient-boosted decision trees like LightGBM/XGBoost/CatBoost, or similar).

- Experience with a deep learning framework (PyTorch preferred).

- Experience working with distributed data processing or parallel compute frameworks (Spark preferred; Ray/Dask or similar).

- Experience with ML lifecycle tooling for training orchestration, experimentation, and model monitoring (e.g., Kubeflow, Airflow, MLflow, or equivalent internal platforms).

- Proficient in using AI-powered developer tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) to accelerate iteration, debugging, and code quality as part of day-to-day development workflows.

- You have mastered taking a simple problem or business scenario into a solution that interacts with multiple software components, and executing on it by writing clear, easily understood, well tested and extensible code.

- You are comfortable navigating a large code base, debugging others' code, and providing feedback to other engineers through code reviews.

- Your experience demonstrates that you take ownership of your growth, proactively seeking feedback from your team, your manager, and your stakeholders.

- You have strong verbal and written communication skills that support effective collaboration with our global engineering team.

Pay Grade - L
Equity Grade - 5
